label: Even Maker
meaning: Your stages are in balance. No single area is dominant and no single area is holding you back. This is the profile of a maker whose practice is coherent across capture, thinking, and making.
bestNextMove: Use the Memo to pick the subdomain with the lowest score and sharpen that one area without disrupting the rest.

label: The Collector
detectionRule: Download leads by 12+ over median AND 18+ over lowest
meaning: You capture and notice more than most. The raw material is there. The gap is what happens after capture — shaping ideas and making things tends to trail what you've gathered.
bestNextMove: Move one captured idea all the way through Idea and Creation this month. Not several — one. Prove the pipeline works end to end.

label: The Thinker
detectionRule: Idea leads by 12+ over median AND 18+ over lowest
meaning: You develop and pressure-test ideas further than most people will. The thinking is strong. The gap is what feeds the thinking (capture) and what happens after it (making).
bestNextMove: Pick a current idea and set a hard build date. Stop refining the thinking and start exposing the idea to the real work of making it.

label: The Builder
detectionRule: Creation leads by 12+ over median AND 18+ over lowest
meaning: You make things. You finish and release where others stall. The risk is that you're building on thin material — not enough capture feeding in, not enough testing of whether the idea is right before you commit.
bestNextMove: Before the next build, spend a week in Download and Idea only. Capture widely. Pressure-test the next idea properly. Your building skill deserves better input.

label: Stuck Upstream
meaning: Your capture and thinking are working. Your making is not. Ideas stay ideas. This is the most common maker pattern and also the most fixable — the gap is almost always about first-step-size, not ability.
bestNextMove: Pick the smallest possible first version of one idea and release it this week. The goal is to move through Creation once, badly, rather than think your way to a perfect version.

label: Stuck Midstream
meaning: You capture and you build — but the middle is skipped. This often produces work that starts well and then stops making sense, because the thinking wasn't done before the making began.
bestNextMove: Before your next build, write a concept note that answers three questions: what is this, who is it for, and what's the real problem underneath it. Then check whether the build still makes sense.

label: Rushed Maker
meaning: You think and you make. What you're missing is raw material. When Download is low, every new idea has to come from somewhere else — usually your own head, under pressure.
bestNextMove: Set up one capture channel this week and use it for fifteen days. Don't organise. Don't synthesise. Just catch what you notice. The input will change what you can make.

label: Developing Maker
detectionRule: Fallback — uneven profile that doesn't fit a named pattern
meaning: Your profile is uneven but not sharply — you have real strengths and real gaps, but none dominant enough to name a single pattern. This is a common phase for someone whose practice is actively shifting.
bestNextMove: Read the subdomain scores rather than the stage scores. Your actual next move sits at subdomain level, not stage level.
